feature: Rollback awareness + bober rollback CLI command
description: Implement `bober rollback <incidentId>` CLI command that reads .bober/incidents/<id>/changelog.jsonl, identifies executed-but-not-rolled-back ChangeEntries, presents the proposed rollback sequence (inverses in reverse order), and executes them via the deployer agent with each rollback step gated as a risky action (Sprint 20 gate applies).

successCriteria.description: src/incident/rollback.ts exports: planRollback(incidentId): Promise<RollbackPlan> reads changelog.jsonl, filters status='executed' AND not status='rolled-back', returns inverses in reverse-execution order.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read file. Test: changelog with 3 executed entries → plan has 3 inverses in reverse order.

successCriteria.description: executeRollback(plan): runs each inverse via the deployer agent's same execution discipline. EACH inverse goes through the Sprint 20 risky-action gate. Updates ChangeEntry status to 'rolled-back' on success.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read execution. Verify each inverse passes through the gate.

successCriteria.description: CLI command `bober rollback <incidentId>` (a) calls planRollback, (b) presents the plan to the user (always presents — even in autopilot mode, this is a destructive op), (c) on confirmation calls executeRollback, (d) writes rollback outcome to .bober/incidents/<id>/rollback-execution.jsonl.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read command. Test: with a fixture incident, run `bober rollback`, verify plan presentation and execution path.

successCriteria.description: Partial rollback support: `bober rollback <incidentId> --since <changeId>` rolls back only changes executed after the named change. `--dry-run` flag prints the plan without executing.

Rollback failure handling: if an inverse fails its postcondition, STOP the rollback sequence, mark that ChangeEntry's status='rolled-back-failed', escalate via checkpoint with all remaining unrolled changes listed.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Test: simulate inverse failure, verify rollback halts and escalates with remaining plan.

successCriteria.description: ChangeEntry without an inverse field (legacy data, or malformed) is treated as un-rollbackable: planRollback excludes it from the plan and surfaces it in a warning section. Sprint 19's requirement that inverse is required at write time should prevent this case in practice, but be defensive.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Test: changelog with one no-inverse entry, verify plan excludes it and warning surfaced.

successCriteria.description: Unit + integration tests cover: planRollback with various changelogs, executeRollback happy-path, --since filter, --dry-run, rollback halts on inverse failure, no-inverse handling.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Locate tests. Run npm run test.

expectedChanges.description: Add `bober rollback` subcommand with --since and --dry-run flags

evaluatorNotes:
Verify per-step gating: a test that executes a 3-step rollback must invoke the gate 3 times. If the test passes with only 1 gate invocation, the plan-level approval is being used as a per-step approval — that's the dangerous design we're explicitly avoiding.

Verify rollback halts CORRECTLY on inverse failure. A 5-step rollback where step 3 fails must NOT execute step 4 and step 5. The test must assert: step 1 'rolled-back', step 2 'rolled-back', step 3 'rolled-back-failed', step 4 'executed' (unchanged), step 5 'executed' (unchanged), and escalation triggered with remaining steps listed.

Verify --dry-run does NOT execute anything: a test must execute a rollback with --dry-run, then verify zero ChangeEntry status updates. NO side effects.

Verify the no-inverse warning is loud — silent skipping of un-rollbackable changes is a footgun. The warning must appear in the plan presentation AND in the audit log AND on stderr.

Verify --since filter is timestamp-based or changeId-based but consistent — pick one and document. A user passing --since with a non-existent changeId should get a clear error, not silent inclusion of everything.

Integration test for the gate interplay: rollback in autopilot mode (allowAutopilotRiskyActions=false) — must invoke disk mechanism per step. Rollback with allowAutopilotRiskyActions=true — auto-approves each step BUT still writes audit entries.
